lines.text: {Transcriber's Note: Rachel has two friends that are not named, so I referred to them as Friend No. 1 and Friend No. 2.}
Central Perk, the whole gang is there including Janice.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Janice has a question. Who of the six of you has sleep with the six of you?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow, it's like a dirty math problem.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry the answer there would be...none of us.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Come on over the years none of you ever y'know, got drunk and stupid.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, that's really a different question.

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: I'm sorry I find it hard to believe that a group of people who spends as much time together as you guys do has never bumped uglies.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, there was that one time that Monica and Rachel got together.

lines.speaker: Monica and Rachel
lines.text: What?!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Excuse me, there was no time!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, but let's say there was. How might that go?

lines.speaker: Janice
lines.text: Okay, okay, well then answer me this. Has any of you ever.... almost?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Does anybody need more coffee?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, I'll take some.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, there's a dog out there!
Opening Credits
Monica and Phoebe's, three years earlier, Phoebe, Monica, and Ross are there

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, that is so unfortunate.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Cute naked guy is really starting to put on weight.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering from bedroom) Okay, I'll be back in just a minute. Oh, Phoebe I'm sorry that I left lipstick marks on the phone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You didn't leave lipstick marks on the phone.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, then it must've been you. Bye. (leaves)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (angrily) Bye-bye! (to Ross) That's why I moved out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, y'know while we're on that, when are you gonna tell my sister that you don't live here anymore.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I think on some levels she already knows.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, she doesn't know that you sneak out every night, she doesn't know that you sneak back every morning, and she doesn't know that you've been living with your Grandmother's for a week now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, well maybe not on those levels.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering, with a goatee) Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm never gonna find a roommate, ever.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why, nobody good?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well let's see, there was the guy with the ferrets, that's plural. The spitter. Oh-ho, and yes, the guy that enjoyed my name so much he felt the need to make a little noise every time he said it. Nice to meet you, Chandler Bing Bing! Great apartment Chandler Bing, Bing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So how many more do you have tomorrow?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Two. This photographer, who seemed really dull. And this actor guy, who I'm not sure about, because when he called and I answered the phone 'Chandler Bing,' he said 'Whoa-whoa, short message.'

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Ross (who has his foot on the coffee table), foot on the floor or come over no more!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Phoebe) Sure, your dresser is missing but this she notices.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I have to go. Yeah, Carol should be home by now, soo...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Umm, how's it going with you guys?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, better, actually. Y'know I-I-I think I finally figured out why we were having so much trouble lately.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, really?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, y'know how I have you guys, well she doesn't really have any close friends that are just hers, but last week she meet this woman at the gym, Susan something, and they really hit it off, and I-I-I think it's gonna make a difference
Chandler's, Chandler is interviewing a potential roommate.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Soo, ah, Eric, what kind of photography do ya do?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh, mostly fashion, so there may be models here from time to time, I hope that's cool.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, that is cool. Because I have models here y'know......never.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Oh, yeah, during the summer, I spend most weekends at my sister's beach house, which you are welcome to use by the way. Although, I should probably tell you, she's a porn star. (Chandler breaks his pencil in half)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, listen I ah, still have one more person to ah meet, but unless it turns out to be your sister, I think you're chances are pretty good. (Eric offers to shake hands) All right. (Chandler hugs him.)
Chandler's, Chandler is interviewing Joey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (running around the apartment pointing out things) Bedroom. Bathroom. Living room. This right here is the kitchen, and thanks for coming by, (opens door) Bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't you ah, don't you wanna ask me any questions?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure. Ummm. What's up?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, ah, I'm an actor. I'm fairly neat. I ah, I got my own TV. Oh, and don't worry I'm totally okay with the gay thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What gay thing?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ah, y'know just in general people being gay, thing. I'm totally cool with that.
the hallway, Monica is coming up the stairs.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well okay Jerry, thanks for stopping by.
Joey is leaving and notices Monica, as Monica notices him

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!
Joey leaves and Monica mouths to Chandler 'Oh my God!'
A bar, Chandler is entering.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, Mon.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey. You wanna hear something that sucks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do I ever.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chris says they're closing down the bar.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No way!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, apparently they're turning it into some kinda coffee place.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Just coffee! Where are we gonna hang out now?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Got me.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to bartender) Can I get a beer.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, did you pick a roommate?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You betcha!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is it the Italian guy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Um-mm, yeah right!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: He's so cute.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yes, and that's what I want a roommate that I can walk around with and be referred to as the funny one.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh look, the pool table's free. Rack 'em up. I'll be back in just a minute. Get ready for me to whip your butt.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, but after that, we're shootin' some pool.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (sitting at a table with some of her friends) (to waitress) Oh, um, no, no, no, no excuse me, hello. Hi. My friend ordered an onion, not an olive, and uh I ordered a rum and Diet Coke, which I don't think this is.

lines.speaker: Waitress
lines.text: I am so sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That's all right. (to her friends) I mean hard is it to get a couple drinks right, huh?
Friend No. 1: Well, I would like to propose a toast to the woman, who in one year from today, become Mrs. Dr. Barry Farber DDS

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ummm, I think it's time to see the ring again. (holds her hand out and they all scream)
Friend No. 2: Oh, isn't it exciting, I mean it's like having a boyfriend for life.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I know.
Friend No. 1: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I don't know. Well maybe it's just the idea of Barry for the rest of my life. I don't know I think I feel like I need to have one last fling, y'know, just to sorta get it out of my system. (Chandler is listening in very intensely)
Friend No. 1: Rachel stop!
Friend No. 2: You're so bad!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm serious, I really, I think I need just to have some...meaningless, sex y'know, with the next guy that I see.
Chandler throws the cue ball under there table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Excuse, I seem to have dropped my ball.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, so?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (picks it up) And now I've picked it up again. (walks over to Monica.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, I went to high school with her. (to Rachel) Rachel! Hi!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Monica! Look! Hi! What do ya think? (shows her, her ring)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, you can't even see where the Titanic hit it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, his name is Barry, he's a doctor, thank you very much.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Awww, just like you always wanted. Congratulations

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Thank you. So how-how 'bout you, are-are you seeing anybody?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aww, not right now.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, but that's okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.
An awkward silence

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, I'll get-get back to my friend.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah, sure, sure, sure, sure. (points at Chandler, who holds up the cue ball as a 'Remember me?' thing) Listen, can we please have lunch the next time I'm in the city?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, that'd be great.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Bye!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Bye! (to Chandler) Ten bucks says, I never see that woman again in my life.
Monica and Phoebe's, Ross is on the phone, as Phoebe is walking by carrying a lamp.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No real-, honey, really it's fine, just g-go with Susan. Really, I, no, I think girls night out is a great idea. Okay, okay, bye

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So what are they doing?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know, something girlie.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Monica, who's entering) Hey, you're early.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you doing with the lamp?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm just taking it to be re-wired.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, well don't take it to the same place you took the stereo, 'cause they've had that thing for over a week.
There is a knock on the door, Phoebe answers it, its Mr. Heckles

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, no, Mr. Heckles no one is making any noise up here.

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: You're disturbing my oboe practice.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don't play the oboe!

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: I could play the oboe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Then I'm gonna have to ask you to keep it down. (slams the door in his face.)
in the hallway, Eric is moving in

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: (to Eric) Who are you?

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: Hi, I'm Eric, I'm gonna be Chandler's new roommate.

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: I'm Chandler's new roommate.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: I-I-I don't think so.

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: I could be Chandler's new roommate.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: But, he told me over the phone.

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: He told me in person.

lines.speaker: Eric
lines.text: That's weird.

lines.speaker: Mr. Heckles
lines.text: Well, I'm going to go into my new apartment now. (goes over to the door and opens it) Ehh! (Eric leaves)
inside Chandler's apartment, Chandler is coming in from his bedroom, sees Mr. Heckles, and screams.
the hallway, Joey is moving in, Monica is leaving.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi, again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! (goes into the apartment)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (leaving to go to work) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you soo, much.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, don't thank me, thank the jerk that never showed up. Okay, I gotta get to get to work.
Joey comes back into the hallway and starts to pick up a heavy box

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You want some help with that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, no thanks, I got it. (picks it up) No I don't!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Whoa! Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whew! Stood up to fast, got a little head rush.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's the heat. (has her hand on his chest, and then pulls it away) And-and the humidity.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's a uh, that's a tough combination.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Do you wanna come in for some lemonade?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Like you wouldn't believe. (they go into the apartment) Wow! This is a great place.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thank you. Just make yourself comfortable.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Gotcha.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: This place is really my Grandmother's. (Joey starts to take off all of his clothes, while Monica gets the glasses and pours the lemonade.) I got it from her when she moved to Florida, otherwise I could never afford a place like this. So if the landlord ever asks, I'm 87 year old woman, who's afraid of her VCR. So are you thirsty?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, you bet I am!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (turning around) Okay, here's your penis!
Commercial Break
continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God!!! What are you doing?!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You said, you wanna come in for some lemonade?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, ah!! We're you just gonna give me some lemonade?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah huh!! Cover yourself up!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh right, right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't believe this! When someone asks you in for lemonade, and to you that means they wanna have sex?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well usually...yeah! Well, not just lemonade, iced tea, sometimes juice. Well, sorry, I just, I thought you liked me. I'm such a jerk.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's okay. I suppose it could happen to anyone, not anyone I know, but... By the way I can still see it.
Monica and Phoebe's, Monica is vacuuming.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Where's your bed?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's not in the apartment? (Monica gives a 'Come on' look) Oh no. I can't believe this is happening again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, enough with the third degree! I-I've, I don't live here anymore.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What are you talking about?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, I-I-I-I don't live here anymore. I-I didn't know how to tell you, but y'know everybody else knows!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Everybody knows!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That was supposed to be a good thing, I forget why. Just listen, Monica, I, do you know, okay, do you know, I couldn't sleep for like a month because I got like a dot of ink on one of the sofa cushions.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, you-you coulda just turned the cushion over.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, I would've except I had a big spaghetti stain on the other side.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?!?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, this is what I'm talking about, this. I-I need to live in a land where people can spill.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You can spill. In the sink.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Aw, honey it's not your fault, y'know this is who you are, and I love you, and I want us to be friends, and if I keep living here I don't see that happening.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I love you, too.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Aww, good. (they hug) What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? I'm just said.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No you're not, you're wondering which cushion it is.
Chandler and Joey's, (now) Joey is watching Baywatch, as Chandler enters from his bedroom.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So ah, whatcha watching?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Baywatch.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What's it about?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Lifeguards.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it sounds kinda stupid... (looks at the TV) Who's she?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nicole Eggert. You'll like her.
Baywatch goes into one of those running scenes.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow! Look at them run.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: They do that a lot. Hey, you want a beer?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I'll go get one.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no, don't get up, I got a cooler right here.
Monica's, Monica is coming out of the bathroom wearing nothing but a towel, as Chandler is entering.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, hello!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Do you have any beers? We're out of beers.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (all depressed) Help yourself.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You okay?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe moved out.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I don't understand, I mean am I so hard to live, is this why I don't have a boyfriend?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Noo!! You don't have a boyfriend because....I don't, I don't know why you don't have a boyfriend. You should have a boyfriend.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I think so.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-ho, come here. (goes and hugs her) Listen, you are one of my favourite people and the most beautiful woman I've ever known in real life.
the bar, Ross is entering, Phoebe is at the bar, they are the only two in the place.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (all depressed) Hi. Where is everybody?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's already closed, Chris gave me the keys to lock up-what is wrong?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My marriage, I think my marriage is um, is kinda over.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no! Why?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: 'Cause Carol's a lesbian. (Phoebe is shocked) And, and I'm not one. And apparently it's not a mix and match situation.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! I don't believe it! Oh, you poor bunny.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sets out a bunch of shot glasses and starts to poor himself a drink, many drinks) I'm an idiot. I mean shoulda seen it, I mean Carol and I'd be out and she'd, she'd see some beautiful woman, and, and she'd be Ross y'know look at her, and I'd think, God, my wife is cool!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Aw! Hey, do you think that Susan person is her lover?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, now I do!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Seven years. I mean we've been together seven years, she's the only woman who's ever loved me, and the only woman I've-I've ever....

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Aw, God Ross. Oh.(goes over and hugs him)
Monica's, Chandler and Monica are still hugging each other.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Umm, this is nice.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know, it is isn't it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, I mean it, this feels really good. Is it a hundred percent cotton?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah! And I got it on sale, too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Anyway, I should go, one of the lifeguards was just about to dismantle a nuclear device.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, if you wanna get a drink later we can.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, that sounds great. (starts to leave) Oh, and listen, it's, it's gonna be....

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I know. Thanks. (Chandler leaves)
the bar, Phoebe is still hugging Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Maybe this would've happened if I'd been more nurturing, or I'd paid more attention, or I... had a uterus. I can't believe this!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I know no, no, y'know you don't deserve this, you don't Ross. You're, you're really, you're so good. (kisses him on the cheek)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And you're so sweet. (kisses him on the other cheek) And you're kind (kisses him on the lips)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks. (kisses her on the lips)
They pause, and they the start kissing passionately, and taking off each others clothes, and they start to lie down on the pool table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait, wait, wait.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Huh?
Ross tries to clear off the pool table by knocking the balls to the other end of the table, but they all bounce back, and he frantically starts to throw them into the pockets.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, it's okay.
Phoebe jumps on to the table and lays down, Ross follows her and hits his head on the light hanging over the pool table.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. (they start kissing again)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My foot is stuck in the pocket.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, I can't get it out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, that's not something a girl wants to hear.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, come on don't start. (they start kissing again) Ouch!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Stupid balls are in the way. (holds up two balls)
They both look at each other and start laughing (Lisa almost lost it there), and sit up. Ross hits his head on the lamp again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh well. It probably would've been the most constructive solution.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You have chalk on your face.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh? (the rest of the gang enters)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Ross you're right, I don't know why I always thought this was real grass.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, are you okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My wife's a lesbian.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cool!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ross-Joey, Joey-Ross. (they shake hands)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi.
Closing Credits
the bar, Chandler is playing pool, as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe you came back.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Don't say anything. I don't wanna speak, I don't wanna think. I just want you to take me and kiss me and make love to me right here, right now.
She hits the jukebox Fonzy style, and It's That Time of Season starts to play, as they start to kiss.
Friend No. 2: Rachel! Rachel! (stirs Rachel from her dream, she's in her car driving back from the city

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?
Friend No. 2: You missed the exit!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, sorry.
Friend No. 1: My God, what were you thinking about?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, (shyly) Barry.

lines.speaker: Her Friends
lines.text: Awwww!!
End
